violations.text: At the time of inspection, the following food items were observed held in refrigeration above 41 degrees Fahrenheit... - Milk and Cream in the front low boy cooler were observed held at 57 degrees Fahrenheit. Milk and Cream were voluntarily disposed of at the time of inspection. - All food items in the back-ups stacked 2 door cooler were observed at 56-58 degrees Fahrenheit. Per the PIC, the food items were recently stocked from the walk-in cooler. Food items moved back to the walk-in cooler. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/31/2025

The following equipment was observed in need of repair/adjustment... - The front low boy cooler was observed at 57 degrees Fahrenheit - The backups 2 door cooler in the back area was observed at 60 degrees Fahrenheit Coolers are not to be used for storage of TCS Foods until properly adjusted/repaired and maintaining an ambient temperature of 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below. New Violation. Correct By: 07/31/2025

Sliced Cheese was observed held at 47 degrees Fahrenheit in the cold unit below the sandwich prep counter. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Based on time out of the walk-in cooler and temperature, Cheese was moved to the walk-in cooler at the time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 05/29/2024
At the time of inspection, Freezer door gasket(s) were observed in poor repair. Per the PIC, gaskets are due for adjustment/replacement. New Violation. Correct By: 05/29/2024

The non-food contact surfaces of the freezer and refrigeration units were observed to have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. Non-FOOD-CONTACT SURFACES of EQUIPMENT shall be cleaned at a frequency necessary to preclude accumulation of soil residues. New Violation. Correct By: 06/01/2024

At the time of inspection, the water supply line to the wall mounted sanitizer/detergent dispensers was observed to be operating improperly for efficient use of the dispenser system. Per the PIC, call to be made for service same day. New Violation. Correct By: 05/29/2024

The outside dumpster was not covered. Receptacles and waste handling units for refuse, recyclables, and returnables shall be kept covered. Covers/Door was shut at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/29/2024

Items were observed in the hand-washing sink in the ware-washing area and were blocked in the front service area by a broom, dust pan, and trash can. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/29/2023

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (ice machine) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Ice machine was taken out of service at the time of inspection and ice was voluntarily disposed of by PIC. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/29/2023

Quaternary ammonium sanitizer solution was in excess of 400 parts per million (ppm) at the 3-bay sink. A quaternary ammonia sanitizer solution applied to food contact surfaces must be applied at the strength as indicated on the manufacturers' label. Service request was made at the time of inspection to repair sanitizer concentration. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/29/2023

The pre-flush spray hose at the 3-bay sink and the hose at the mop sink is hanging below the flood rim. A plumbing system shall be installed to prevent backflow of a liquid or gas contaminant into the water supply by providing an air gap or installing an approved backflow prevention device. New Violation. Correct By: 08/29/2023
The outside dumpster is not covered. Receptacles and waste handling units for refuse, recyclables, and returnables shall be kept covered. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/12/2023
